pending.body: finding_summary: `check_beads_health()` never validates that `.beads/issues.jsonl` is parseable JSONL, so truncated or otherwise malformed exports are classified as `Healthy` and the new mutation guards still allow `br update/create/close` to run against corrupted bead state. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_health.rs", "src/cli/project.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs", "src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs"]. evidence: `src/adapters/br_health.rs:44-70` only checks file readability, conflict markers, and `br` availability; the new guard sites in `src/cli/project.rs:965-974`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2998-3020`, and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:117-123,221-223` trust that result before mutating beads. remediation_hint: treat malformed JSONL as unhealthy by parsing each line (or performing an equivalent bounded `br`-backed validation) before returning `Healthy`.

finding_summary: the new health-check path can hang bead mutations indefinitely because `check_beads_health()` calls `BrAdapter::check_available()`, and that probe uses blocking `std::process::Command::output()` with no timeout. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_health.rs", "src/adapters/br_process.rs"]. evidence: `src/adapters/br_health.rs:18-20,91-95` invokes `BrAdapter::new().check_available()` on every health check, and `src/adapters/br_process.rs:386-389` runs `br --version` via `output()` instead of the timeout-governed process runner used elsewhere. remediation_hint: run the availability probe through the existing timed process path (or cache a startup check) so a wedged `br` binary cannot stall claim/close/create flows forever.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 6 -> Plan and Implement
